summary: Partial hard-fraud benchmark completed: plugin outperformed no-plugin; automl was not executed because AutoGluon is unavailable and the documented hard-attrition scenario is missing from this checkout.

findings.title: Stale benchmark scenario reference
findings.description: The benchmark skill defaults ambiguous runs to hard-attrition, but demo/scenarios/hard-attrition is not present in this repository checkout.
findings.fix: Restore the hard-attrition demo assets or update benchmark-e2e to point at the current scenario set before relying on the default auto path.

findings.title: AutoML dependency missing
findings.description: The automl mode could not run because the autogluon package is not installed in the current environment.
findings.fix: Install AutoGluon with uv before re-running benchmark-e2e with the automl mode enabled.

findings.title: Schema and README are out of sync
findings.description: Repository docs still mention older benchmark scenario labels while the skill instructions now use hard-fraud and hard-attrition.
findings.fix: Align README, schemas.md, and the benchmark-e2e skill so users see one consistent benchmark contract.

recommendation.default_mode_rationale: For the fraud scenario, the plugin path catches the same core blockers as manual review while adding stronger target framing, better metric selection, and a more reproducible remediation plan.
recommendation.fallback_mode_rationale: The manual path still reaches a correct no-go on the fraud dataset, but it is less structured and weaker on auditability than the plugin path.
